free_text: (n,p),(n,a) and (n,2n) reaction cross-sections for
some isotopes of Zr,Pd and Cd at 14.8 MeV
first author
Consisted of disc-shaped metalic foils of Cd,Zr,Pd
with natural isotopic composition,99.8% purity
Used for
determination of neutron flux, which was further tested
with the Fe-56 (n,p) reaction.
(Agreement with sigma=102.mb(ENDF/B-4) was found).
The neutron flux was monitored continuously with a
fission chamber behind the sample, in order to
correct for variations in neutron flux with time.
The time averaged neutron fluxes at the center
of the sample in the two irradiations, determined
from the Al data, were respectively
The values obtained from the Fe data agreed well (to
about 1%) with these results.
They include:
-the statistical error of the measured intensity
-the error in the counting efficiency
-the error of the flux measurement,and
-the error in the irradiated mass
Errors in the nuclear data used, such as abundances
and half-lives, were not taken into consideration.
The recommended value for a given reaction has been
computed as the weighted average of the results taken
over two irradiations and the different gamma-ray
energies.
Dead-time and random pile-up corrections in most cases
amounted to less than 10%. Corrections for real
coincidence summing were computed using the known
decay-schemes and the total counting efficiency of the
detector. The maximum value of this correction was 2%.
In most of the cases the absorption and self-absorption
corrections were less than 7%.
Data taken from Appl.Radiat.Isot.38(1987)p.989,
Tab.2, Goncalves
MR-
DG.-New reference added.-
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Cosmetic changed, MONITOR description was corrected,
MONIT-REF was added. Reaction codes in 004, 006:
(n,p) -> (n,x).  See description in REACTION.
The cross-section result for this reaction includes a
contribution from the Zr-91((n,d)+(n,np)+(n,pn))Y-90-m
reaction.This contribution has been estimated to amount
to about 0.3 mb
SD: REACTION code was changed:
the cross-section result for this reaction include a
contribution from the 92Zr((n,d)+(n,np)+(n,pn))91mY
reaction.However,an estimation of this contribution
could not be made
SD: REACTION code was changed:
the cross-section result for this reaction includes a
very small contribution of the 116Cd(n,2n)115mCd
reaction because the 115mCd nucleus also emits the
336.2 keV and 492 keV gamma-lines. This contribution
however is less than the experimental error
